children.heading: Establishment
children.label: a Establishment
children.text: During each of the 2002 through 2031 fiscal years, the Secretary shall provide payments to producers that enter into contracts with the Secretary under the program.

children.heading: Practices
children.label: 1 Practices
children.text: A contract under the program may apply to the performance of one or more practices.

children.heading: Term
children.label: 2 Term
children.text: A contract under the program shall have a term that does not exceed 10 years.

children.heading: Bidding down
children.label: c Bidding down
children.text: If the Secretary determines that the environmental values of two or more applications for payments are comparable, the Secretary shall not assign a higher priority to the application only because it would present the least cost to the program.

children.heading: Availability of payments
children.label: 1 Availability of payments
children.text: Payments are provided to a producer to implement one or more practices under the program.

children.label: A
children.text: 75 percent of the costs associated with planning, design, materials, equipment, installation, labor, management, maintenance, or training;

children.label: B
children.text: 100 percent of income foregone by the producer; or

children.label: i
children.text: 75 percent of the costs incurred for those elements covered under subparagraph (A); and

children.label: ii
children.text: 100 percent of income foregone for those elements covered under subparagraph (B).

children.heading: Special rule involving payments for foregone income
children.label: 3 Special rule involving payments for foregone income
A
children.text: soil health;

children.label: B
children.text: water quality and quantity improvement;

children.label: C
children.text: nutrient management;

children.label: D
children.text: pest management;

children.label: E
children.text: air quality improvement;

children.label: F
children.text: wildlife habitat development, including pollinator habitat; or

children.label: G
children.text: invasive species management.

children.label: i
children.text: to not more than 90 percent of the costs associated with planning, design, materials, equipment, installation, labor, management, maintenance, or training; and

children.label: ii
children.text: to not less than 25 percent above the otherwise applicable rate.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: On an election by a producer described in subparagraph (A), the Secretary shall provide at least 50 percent of the amount determined under subparagraph (A) in advance for all costs related to purchasing materials or contracting.

children.heading: Return of funds
children.label: ii Return of funds
children.text: If funds provided in advance are not expended during the 90-day period beginning on the date of receipt of the funds, the funds shall be returned within a reasonable timeframe, as determined by the Secretary.

children.label: I
children.text: notify each producer described in subparagraph (A), at the time of enrollment in the program, of the option to receive advance payments under clause (i); and

children.label: II
children.text: document the election of each producer described in subparagraph (A) to receive advance payments under clause (i) with respect to each practice that has costs described in that clause.

children.heading: Financial assistance from other sources
children.label: 5 Financial assistance from other sources
children.text: Except as provided in paragraph (6), any payments received by a producer from a State or private organization or person for the implementation of one or more practices on eligible land of the producer shall be in addition to the payments provided to the producer under this subsection.

children.heading: Other payments
children.label: 6 Other payments
children.text: A producer shall not be eligible for payments for practices on eligible land under the program if the producer receives payments or other benefits for the same practice on the same land under another program under this subchapter.

children.label: i
children.text: addresses specific causes of impairment relating to excessive nutrients in groundwater or surface water;

children.label: ii
children.text: addresses the conservation of water to advance drought mitigation and declining aquifers;

children.label: iii
children.text: meets other environmental priorities and other priority resource concerns identified in habitat or other area restoration plans; or

children.label: iv
children.text: is geographically targeted to address a natural resource concern in a specific watershed.

children.heading: Increased payments
children.label: B Increased payments
children.text: Notwithstanding paragraph (2), in the case of a practice designated under subparagraph (A), the Secretary may increase the amount that would otherwise be provided for a practice under this subsection to not more than 90 percent of the costs associated with planning, design, materials, equipment, installation, labor, management, maintenance, or training.

children.heading: Modification or termination of contracts
children.label: e Modification or termination of contracts
A
children.text: the producer agrees to the modification or termination; and

children.label: B
children.text: the Secretary determines that the modification or termination is in the public interest.

children.heading: Involuntary termination
children.label: 2 Involuntary termination
children.text: The Secretary may terminate a contract under the program if the Secretary determines that the producer violated the contract.

children.heading: Livestock
children.label: 1 Livestock
children.text: For each of fiscal years 2019 through 2023, at least 50 percent of the funds made available for payments under the program shall be targeted at practices relating to livestock production, including grazing management practices.

children.heading: Fiscal years 2014 through 2018
children.label: A Fiscal years 2014 through 2018
children.text: For each of fiscal years 2014 through 2018, at least 5 percent of the funds made available for payments under the program shall be targeted at practices benefitting wildlife habitat under subsection (g).

children.heading: Fiscal years 2019 through 2031
children.label: B Fiscal years 2019 through 2031
children.text: For each of fiscal years 2019 through 2031, at least 10 percent of the funds made available for payments under the program shall be targeted at practices benefitting wildlife habitat under subsection (g).

children.label: A
children.text: upland wildlife habitat;

children.label: B
children.text: wetland wildlife habitat;

children.label: C
children.text: habitat for threatened and endangered species;

children.label: D
children.text: fish habitat;

children.label: E
children.text: habitat on pivot corners and other irregular areas of a field; and

children.label: F
children.text: other types of wildlife habitat, as determined by the Secretary.

children.heading: State technical committee
children.label: 2 State technical committee
children.text: In determining the practices eligible for payment under paragraph (1) and targeted for funding under subsection (f), the Secretary shall consult with the relevant State technical committee not less often than once each year.

children.heading: Maximum term
children.label: 3 Maximum term
children.text: In the case of a contract under the program entered into solely for the establishment of 1 or more annual management practices for the benefit of wildlife as described in paragraph (1), notwithstanding any maximum contract term established by the Secretary, the contract shall have a term that does not exceed 10 years.

children.label: A
children.text: a practice to carry out postharvest flooding; or

children.label: B
children.text: a practice to maintain the hydrology of temporary and seasonal wetlands of not more than 2 acres to maintain waterfowl and migratory bird habitat on working cropland.

children.heading: Water conservation or irrigation efficiency practice
children.label: h Water conservation or irrigation efficiency practice
A
children.text: water conservation scheduling, water distribution efficiency, soil moisture monitoring, or an appropriate combination thereof;

children.label: B
children.text: irrigation-related structural or other measures that conserve surface water or groundwater, including managed aquifer recovery practices; or

children.label: C
children.text: a transition to water-conserving crops, water-conserving crop rotations, or deficit irrigation.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Notwithstanding , the Secretary may enter into a contract under this subsection with a State, irrigation district, groundwater management district, acequia, land-grant mercedes, or similar entity under a streamlined contracting process to implement water conservation or irrigation practices under a watershed-wide project that will effectively conserve water, provide fish and wildlife habitat, or provide for drought-related environmental mitigation, as determined by the Secretary.section 1308(f)(6) of title 7

children.label: i
children.text: eligible land of a producer; or

children.label: I
children.text: under the control of an irrigation district, groundwater management district, acequia, land-grant mercedes, or similar entity; and

children.label: II
children.text: adjacent to eligible land described in clause (i), as determined by the Secretary.

children.heading: Waiver authority
children.label: C Waiver authority
children.text: The Secretary may waive the applicability of the limitations in or for a payment made under a contract entered into under this paragraph if the Secretary determines that the waiver is necessary to fulfill the objectives of the project.section 1308–3a(b) of title 7section 3839aa–7 of this title

children.heading: Contract limitations
children.label: D Contract limitations
children.text: If the Secretary grants a waiver under subparagraph (C), the Secretary may impose a separate payment limitation for the contract with respect to which the waiver applies.

children.label: A
children.text: consistent with the law of the State in which the land on which the practices will be implemented is located, there is a reduction in water use in the operation on that land; or

children.label: B
children.text: except in the case of an application under paragraph (2), the producer agrees not to use any associated water savings to bring new land, other than incidental land needed for efficient operations, under irrigated production, unless the producer is participating in a watershed-wide project that will effectively conserve water, as determined by the Secretary.

children.heading: Effect
children.label: 4 Effect
children.text: Nothing in this subsection authorizes the Secretary to modify the process for determining the annual allocation of funding to States under the program.

children.heading: Payments for conservation practices related to organic production
children.label: i Payments for conservation practices related to organic production
A
children.text: to organic production; and

children.label: B
children.text: to the transition to organic production.

children.label: A
children.text: to develop and carry out an organic system plan; or

children.label: B
children.text: to develop and implement conservation practices for certified organic production that are consistent with an organic system plan and the purposes of this subpart.

children.label: I
children.text: $20,000 per year; or

children.label: II
children.text: $80,000 during any 6-year period; and

children.label: ii
children.text: during the period of fiscal years 2019 through 2023, $140,000.

children.heading: Technical assistance
children.label: B Technical assistance
children.text: In applying the limitations under subparagraph (A), the Secretary shall not take into account payments received for technical assistance.

children.heading: Exclusion of certain organic certification costs
children.label: 4 Exclusion of certain organic certification costs
children.text: Payments may not be made under this subsection to cover the costs associated with organic certification that are eligible for cost-share payments under .section 6523 of title 7

children.label: A
children.text: is not pursuing organic certification; or

children.label: B
children.text: is not in compliance with the Organic Foods Production Act of 1990 ( et seq).7 U.S.C. 6501

children.heading: Identification of eligible priority resource concerns for States
children.label: 1 Identification of eligible priority resource concerns for States

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Secretary, in consultation with the applicable State technical committee established under , shall identify watersheds (or other appropriate regions or areas within a State) and the corresponding priority resource concerns for those watersheds or other regions or areas that are eligible to be the subject of an incentive contract under this subsection.section 3861(a) of this title

children.heading: Limitation
children.label: B Limitation
children.text: For each of the relevant land uses within the watersheds, regions, or other areas identified under subparagraph (A), the Secretary shall identify not more than 3 eligible priority resource concerns.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: The Secretary shall enter into contracts with producers under this subsection that require the implementation, adoption, management, and maintenance of incentive practices that effectively address at least 1 eligible priority resource concern identified under paragraph (1) for the term of the contract.

children.label: I
children.text: funding, through annual payments, for certain incentive practices to attain increased levels of conservation on eligible land; or

children.label: II
children.text: assistance, through a practice payment, to implement an incentive practice.

children.heading: Term
children.label: B Term
children.text: A contract under this subsection shall have a term of not less than 5, and not more than 10, years.

children.label: i
children.text: give priority to applications that address eligible priority resource concerns identified under paragraph (1); and

children.label: ii
children.text: evaluate applications relative to other applications for similar agriculture and forest operations.

children.label: i
children.text: adopting and installing incentive practices; and

children.label: ii
children.text: managing, maintaining, and improving the incentive practices for the duration of the contract, as determined appropriate by the Secretary.

children.label: i
children.text: the level and extent of the incentive practice to be installed, adopted, completed, maintained, managed, or improved;

children.label: ii
children.text: the cost of the installation, adoption, completion, management, maintenance, or improvement of the incentive practice;

children.label: I
children.text: increased economic risk;

children.label: II
children.text: loss in revenue due to anticipated reductions in yield; and

children.label: III
children.text: economic losses during transition to a resource-conserving cropping system or resource-conserving land use; and

children.label: iv
children.text: the extent to which compensation would ensure long-term continued maintenance, management, and improvement of the incentive practice.

children.label: i
children.text: in the case of annual payments under paragraph (2)(A)(ii)(I), make those payments as soon as practicable after October 1 of each fiscal year for which increased levels of conservation are maintained during the term of the contract; and

children.label: ii
children.text: in the case of practice payments under paragraph (2)(A)(ii)(II), make those payments as soon as practicable on the implementation of an incentive practice.
